candidates.name: discriminable normative state
candidates.reason: deriving admissible evolution, dependency-sensitive revision, historical identity, and uniform recovery requires R2-R5 bridge machinery

candidates.name: constrained information transformation
candidates.reason: content identity, constraint semantics, dependency grounds, lineage, and recovery interface reconstruct R1-R5

candidates.name: counterfactual difference preservation
candidates.reason: cannot distinguish commitment content, historical identity, and grounds without charged annotations

candidates.name: path-sensitive normative distinction
candidates.reason: path labels encode R1, R3, and R4 while admissibility and effective queries encode R2 and R5

candidates.name: effective revision-relevant recoverability
candidates.primitive: effective recovery of every distinction relevant to permissible revision
candidates.reason: its expansion is exactly commitments, admissibility, dependencies, histories, and recovery

candidates.name: typed transition relation
candidates.reason: types and edges alone underdetermine grounds, supersession identity, and uniform bounded recovery

candidates.name: proof-relevant update algebra
candidates.reason: proof objects omit non-proof commitments and require histories and query semantics to satisfy P*

candidates.name: causal intervention substrate
candidates.reason: semantic commitments and normative admissibility are not derivable from causal structure alone

candidates.name: coalgebraic observation kernel
candidates.reason: behavioral equivalence collapses dependency and history distinctions unless RCCD-equivalent observations are added

candidates.name: universal interpreter
candidates.primitive: one interpreter capable of decoding arbitrary encodings
candidates.reason: violates non-vacuity and hides all five components in the interpreter and codebook

paired_distinction_tests: same current state, different revision history
same components, different scheduler-sensitive revision order
